communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I AM REQUESTING BODY WORN CAMERA FOOTAGE FROM THE 03/18/2026 APPROXIAMATELY 4PM SHOOTING OF SEAN BARRY AT WEBB PARK. I AM REQUESTING BODY WORN CAMERA FOOTAGE FROM THE SHOOTING OFFICERS BWC. IF THERE IS NONE FROM THAT OFFICER I AM REQUSTING BWC FOOTAGE FROM ANY OFFICERS BWC THAT CAPTURED THE SHOOTING OR AFTERMATH

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Annemarie Grant

title: BWC & Incident Records: Jeremy Reynolds Multi-County Pursuit, Stolen Cruiser & Standoff – Richland County, OH (March 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the multi-county pursuit, standoff, and stolen cruiser incident involving suspect Jeremy Reynolds.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: March 6, 2022
Location: Richland County, Ohio — Millsboro Road / Lexington-Springmill Road area; U.S. 30; State Route 511
Suspect Name: Jeremy Reynolds, 31
Agencies Involved: Ontario Police Department, Ohio State Highway Patrol
 
Incident Summary: Troopers attempted to stop a suspected stolen vehicle driven by Reynolds. He struck several vehicles during the pursuit, causing his car to break down on Millsboro Road. A 40-minute standoff ensued. Reynolds then escaped using smoke from the disabled vehicle as cover, stole an unlocked Ohio State Highway Patrol cruiser — with a woman still in the back seat — and drove off despite being shot twice. He later abandoned the cruiser, carjacked a minivan, and led officers on a second chase until troopers intentionally rammed the minivan to end the pursuit.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Ontario PD officers and Ohio State Highway Patrol troopers involved in the initial pursuit, standoff, shooting, and second pursuit.
 
All dashcam and in-car camera footage from all patrol vehicles involved.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Sophia Ross DUI Arrest – Sarasota, FL (November 2024)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the DUI arrest of Sophia Ross.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: November 17, 2024
Location: Sarasota, Florida
Suspect Name: Sophia Ross, 22
Arresting Agency: Sarasota Police Department
 
Incident Summary: Officers pulled Ross over after observing her stopping at green lights and striking several curbs. Upon contact, Ross was swaying off balance, slurring her words, and acting lethargic. She admitted to having been drinking at a bar. During the traffic stop, Ross repeatedly called the officer "babe" and "daddy," attempted to kiss the officer, flashed her Hooters uniform top, and tried to flirt her way out of the arrest. She initially refused field sobriety tests saying "I'm literally drinking and not gonna drive" and denied she had been driving. She was arrested and charged with DUI. Her blood alcohol content was 0.318, nearly four times the legal limit.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from all officers who responded to and interacted with Sophia Ross during the traffic stop, field sobriety testing, arrest, and transport.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles present during the stop.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives.
 
All dispatch/CAD logs related to this traffic stop.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings from the jail.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Federal Judge Thomas Ludington DUI Arrest – Emmet County, MI (October 2025)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the DUI arrest of U.S. District Judge Thomas Ludington following a single-vehicle crash.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: October 2025
Location: Emmet County, Michigan
Suspect Name: U.S. District Judge Thomas Ludington, 72
Arresting Agency: Michigan State Police
 
Incident Summary: Judge Ludington, a George W. Bush appointee to the federal bench, crashed his Cadillac and was arrested for operating while intoxicated. His blood alcohol content was 0.27 — more than three times the legal limit of 0.08. Bodycam footage shows Ludington slurring his words, denying having anything to drink, claiming he didn't know how his car crashed or why his airbags deployed, and having difficulty following police instructions. He was charged with OWI and OWI with a BAC of .17 or higher.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from all Michigan State Police troopers who responded to the crash and interacted with Judge Ludington during the investigation, field sobriety testing, arrest, and transport.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Tiger Woods DUI Arrest – Jupiter, FL (May 2017)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the DUI arrest of professional golfer Eldrick "Tiger" Woods.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: May 29, 2017
Location: Military Trail, Jupiter, Palm Beach County, Florida
Suspect Name: Eldrick Tont "Tiger" Woods
Arresting Agency: Jupiter Police Department
 
Incident Summary: In the early morning hours, officers found Woods asleep behind the wheel of his running Mercedes-Benz, which was parked awkwardly on the roadway with two flat tires and damage to the front and rear bumpers. Officers had to wake him. Woods appeared disoriented and failed field sobriety tests. He was arrested for DUI. A breathalyzer showed 0.000 BAC, but toxicology later revealed five prescription drugs in his system including Vicodin, Dilaudid, Xanax, Ambien, and THC. He later pled guilty to reckless driving.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from responding and arresting officers who interacted with Tiger Woods during the traffic encounter, field sobriety testing, arrest, and transport.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles present at the scene.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings from the Palm Beach County Jail.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic
